message:
fix(cli): validate config set values before saving

configSetCommand now validates updates with PartialConfigSchema and returns code=invalid_value instead of persisting an invalid config that would make subsequent loads fall back to defaults.\n\nAlso updates config tests to cover string parsing safely and closes bead bd-1clf.

updated config tests; CLI test suite passes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-1d6d","title":"Remove ultrathink instruction from prompt text","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T02:22:53.539441606Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-31T02:30:42.663176238Z","closed_at":"2026-01-31T02:30:42.662998253Z","close_reason":"Removed ultrathink instructions from prompt content and exports","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-1eho","title":"Feature: Auto-Tagging + Duplicate Detection (Metadata Assistant)","description":"## Background As the prompt library grows, manual tagging becomes inconsistent and duplicates sneak in. Pro users need fast, reliable metadata and dedupe support. ## Scope - Auto-suggest tags, categories, and short descriptions using deterministic rules + semantic similarity (no SKILL.md). - Duplicate/near-duplicate detection with similarity thresholds and preview diffs. - Merge flow: mark as alias, merge tags, or ignore with rationale. - CLI: `jfp tags suggest ` and `jfp dedupe scan` (Pro-only). ## Deliverables - Metadata suggestion service + similarity index. - UI panel for suggestions and duplicate resolution. - CLI scan output with JSON option. - Admin override for global tag mappings. ## Test Requirements ### Unit Tests - Similarity scoring thresholds and tag suggestion rules. - Duplicate detection determinism. ### Integration Tests - Suggestions saved to prompt metadata and reflected in search. - Dedupe merge updates references (packs/workflows/collections). ### E2E Tests - Create prompt -> receive suggestions -> accept -> verify tags. - Detect duplicate -> merge -> verify references updated. ## Success Criteria - Tag consistency improves without manual rework. - Duplicate prompts are surfaced and resolvable. - Search quality improves for Pro users.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IndigoFinch","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:13:48.758255166Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T20:34:31.681132887Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T20:34:31.681111768Z","close_reason":"Admin tag mapping overrides shipped in 0c67683","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-1gf6","title":"Docs: list jfp packs under Premium commands","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T20:15:08.507514242Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T20:15:19.353380482Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T20:15:19.353355546Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1gf6","depends_on_id":"bd-kfuj","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-02-01T20:15:08.507514242Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} @@ -23,6 +24,7 @@ {"id":"bd-2vsu","title":"Feature: Personalized Recommendations Feed (Pro)","description":"## Background Pro users want to discover high‑value prompts quickly without browsing the entire catalog. A personalized feed drives engagement and retention. ## Scope - "For You" feed based on usage signals (views, runs, saves) + explicit preferences. - Explanations: "Because you used X" or "Trending in your category". - Weekly digest email (opt‑in) with top recommendations. - CLI: `jfp recommend` returns a ranked list with rationale. - Prompts/prompt packs only (no SKILL.md or skills content). ## Deliverables - Ranking pipeline and data model for signals. - Feed UI with filters (timeframe, category). - Digest email template + scheduling job. - CLI command with JSON output. ## Test Requirements ### Unit Tests - Ranking weight calculations and deterministic ordering. - Preference filtering and exclusion rules. ### Integration Tests - Signal ingestion -> feed updates -> UI reflects changes. - Digest job generates consistent results. ### E2E Tests - Toggle preferences -> feed updates -> digest preview. - CLI recommend -> matches feed top items. ## Success Criteria - Users find relevant prompts faster. - Feed is explainable and feels trustworthy. - Opt‑in privacy settings are respected.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:14:02.330248162Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:30:55.979101810Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-2yxb","title":"UI: Budget settings page (local-only)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T20:36:36.334511544Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T20:39:09.540885274Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T20:39:09.540865797Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2yxb","depends_on_id":"bd-2auv","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-02-01T20:36:36.334511544Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"bd-397t","title":"Onboarding: codebase archaeology + agent mail intro","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T23:28:30.532748268Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T23:30:11.969556377Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T23:30:11.969538473Z","close_reason":"Completed onboarding: AGENTS/README reread, codebase archaeology summary, Agent Mail registration + intro messages","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} +{"id":"bd-3hxx","title":"Feature: Review sort options (newest, most helpful, oldest)","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-06T00:14:41.504818372Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T00:14:46.292255214Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-3jtd","title":"EPIC: World-Class UI/UX Polish - Mobile & Desktop Optimization","description":"## Overview Hyper-optimize both mobile and desktop UI/UX to achieve world-class visual appeal, polish, and slickness following best practices from industry leaders like Stripe. ## Completed Work 1. ✅ Fixed critical mobile button text truncation in PromptDetailModal - Restructured to mobile-first approach with full-width primary action - Secondary actions in 2x2 grid that never truncates ## Mobile Optimization Goals - Ensure all touch targets are 44px minimum (iOS HIG standard) - Proper safe area insets for notched devices - Bottom-sheet interactions for modals - Optimized category filter horizontal scrolling - Touch-friendly gestures with haptic feedback ## Desktop Optimization Goals - Refined hover states and micro-interactions - Proper keyboard navigation - Consistent typography and spacing - Premium shadow and elevation design - Stripe-level polish on all interactive elements ## Components to Audit - [x] PromptDetailModal - Fixed - [x] Button component - Already well-designed - [x] BottomSheet - Well-designed with safe area support - [x] SpotlightSearch - Well-designed with mobile-first approach - [x] Nav - Good touch targets - [x] BottomNav - Good mobile design - [x] PromptCard - Good Stripe-inspired design ## Status Initial critical bug fixed. Full audit shows components are already well-designed following mobile-first principles.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-21T20:27:47.367791503Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T20:28:41.377830454Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T20:28:41.377778706Z","close_reason":"Completed UI/UX audit. Fixed critical mobile button truncation. All other components already follow mobile-first best practices with proper touch targets, safe area support, and Stripe-inspired design.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-3ppu","title":"Docs: add jfp cost to CLI help overview","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T20:21:05.053649862Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T20:21:34.906947450Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T20:21:34.906922132Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ppu","depends_on_id":"bd-2auv","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-02-01T20:21:05.053649862Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"bd-3tqx","title":"CLI getCurrentUser respects JFP_TOKEN","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-01T17:49:42.042653645Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T17:49:49.357119170Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T17:49:49.357099363Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} @@ -172,7 +174,7 @@ {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9r7s","title":"Add --json flag support for all new CLI commands","description":"Add --json flag support to all new CLI commands for machine-readable output. Commands to Update: - jfp login (output: { authenticated: true, email, tier }) - jfp logout (output: { logged_out: true }) - jfp whoami (output: { email, tier, user_id }) - jfp collections list (output: { collections: [...] }) - jfp collections show (output: { collection, items: [...] }) - jfp notes (output: { prompt_id, notes: [...] }) - jfp save (output: { saved: true, prompt_id }) - jfp list --mine/--saved (output: { prompts: [...] }) Implementation: - Add --json/-j flag to all relevant commands - When --json is set, output only valid JSON to stdout - Errors should also be JSON: { error: true, message, code } - Progress/status messages go to stderr when --json is enabled - Ensure consistent field naming across all commands ","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:23:15.385023105Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T02:20:01.044458985Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T02:20:01.044458985Z","close_reason":"All CLI commands now have --json flag support with consistent output format","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9r7s","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9g76","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:19.154441027Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9r7s","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-cgj5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:08.868781233Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9rgl","title":"Redesign 'Clear All Filters' as a proper button","description":"# Task: Redesign Clear Filters Button ## Current Implementation (page.tsx:165-172) ```tsx {hasActiveFilters && ( )} ``` ## Problems 1. Plain text link - low visual prominence 2. Uses indigo (inconsistent with neutral design system) 3. Only visible when filters active (no affordance) 4. No icon - relies entirely on text ## Proposed Design ### Option A: Always-visible ghost button (Recommended) ```tsx ``` ### Option B: Only when active, but as a proper button ```tsx {hasActiveFilters && ( )} ``` ## Implementation Details ### Add filter count calculation ```tsx const filterCount = useMemo(() => { let count = 0; if (filters.query) count++; if (filters.category) count++; count += filters.tags.length; return count; }, [filters]); ``` ### Add accessibility ```tsx aria-label={`Clear all ${filterCount} active filters`} ``` ## Design Rationale - Ghost button maintains visual hierarchy (filters are secondary to results) - Always visible = discoverable (disabled state indicates "nothing to clear") - Icon + text = clear meaning - Count badge = precise feedback - Matches CategoryFilter/TagFilter clear button pattern ## Testing 1. With no filters: button disabled, grayed out 2. With 1 filter: button enabled, shows "(1)" 3. With multiple: button shows count 4. Click clears all and disables button 5. Keyboard accessible (Tab, Enter) ## Files Modified - apps/web/src/app/page.tsx ## Dependencies - Should use the updated neutral color palette (do after color fix) - Matches pattern already used in TagFilter.tsx (Clear button)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T05:38:18.479565228Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T15:06:14.296451909Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T15:06:14.296451909Z","close_reason":"Implementation verified complete: Button component with ghost variant, X icon, filterCount memo, aria-label, and neutral colors. Build passes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9rgl","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ogel","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T05:43:15.125413399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9sj","title":"Feature: GitHub Actions E2E Integration","description":"Add E2E tests to GitHub Actions CI workflow. Configure CLI E2E tests to run on push/PR. Configure Playwright for web E2E with proper browser setup. Upload test artifacts (screenshots, videos, logs) on failure. Optional: multi-browser test matrix for comprehensive coverage.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-10T22:43:26.454174609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T00:16:26.265863560Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T00:16:26.265863560Z","close_reason":"CI infrastructure implemented in parent EPIC","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9sj","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-945","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T22:53:36.860791733Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9sj","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-jxd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T22:53:26.008125350Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9sj","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-nfm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T22:53:59.405040418Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} -{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9wk9","title":"EPIC: Ratings & Reviews System","description":"## Origin Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-cdp. ## Overview Ratings and reviews provide quality signals for community content, helping users discover the best prompts, packs, and skills while rewarding creators for quality contributions. ## Rating System Design ### Simple Rating (MVP) - Thumbs up / thumbs down (binary) - Easy, low-friction interaction - Clear positive/negative signal - Calculate "approval rate" percentage ### Extended Rating (V2) - 1-5 star rating - Written review (optional) - Helpful/not helpful on reviews - Response from author ## Key Features ### Rate Content - One-click rating on any ratable content - Rate: prompts, packs, skills - Change rating anytime - See your rating history ### View Ratings - Show rating on content cards - Rating breakdown on detail pages - Sort by rating in lists - Filter by minimum rating ### Reviews (V2) - Write text review with rating - Edit/delete own reviews - Report inappropriate reviews - Author can respond to reviews ### Leaderboards - Top rated prompts this week/month - Top contributors by ratings received - Rising stars (new content with high ratings) ## Anti-Gaming Measures - One rating per user per item - Rate limiting on rapid ratings - Flag suspicious patterns - Weight by user reputation ## Database Schema - ratings (id, user_id, content_type, content_id, rating_value, created_at, updated_at) - reviews (id, user_id, content_type, content_id, rating_id, content, created_at) - review_votes (review_id, user_id, is_helpful) ## UI Components - RatingButton (thumbs up/down) - RatingDisplay (show current rating) - RatingBreakdown (detailed stats) - ReviewList (paginated reviews) - ReviewForm (write review) ## Integration Points - Community prompts (Swap Meet) - Prompt packs - Skills - Collections (V2) ","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"SilverPine","created_at":"2026-01-11T06:20:02.532432863Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T06:40:56.237461854Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"comments":[{"id":25,"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9wk9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Code review by agent: MVP implementation is complete and solid. Current state: - RatingButton: Thumbs up/down with haptic feedback, animations, accessible - RatingDisplay: Compact/detailed variants with approval bar - API route: /api/ratings with GET/POST, rate limiting - Rating store: In-memory with proper key generation - useRating hook: SWR pattern with optimistic updates - E2E helpers: ratings-helpers.ts for testing Remaining work per EPIC: 1. Sort by rating in prompt lists 2. Filter by minimum rating 3. Leaderboards (top rated prompts) 4. V2: Written reviews 5. V2: Author responses Note: Rating persistence requires premium repo DB.","created_at":"2026-02-03T06:08:38Z"},{"id":26,"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9wk9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed sort by rating (bd-1kt2): Extended FilterState, SortSelector component, /api/ratings/summaries endpoint, useAllRatings hook.
